prompt: Wide photograph of a finished whole-exterior repaint of a 1920s Methuen MA two-family colonial — clapboard siding painted a soft warm white, shutters and front door in a deep navy, all trim and window casings in crisp white satin. The shot is captured from the sidewalk three-quarter front angle showing two stories and the roof line. Mature street tree on the left edge. Mid-afternoon soft sun. High-contrast, slightly desaturated photography, working-contractor documentary catalog feel — NOT HGTV bright, NOT luxury hotel-staged, NOT real-estate photographer brightness. The lighting in a Snap-On distributor's tool room. No people in frame, no readable text anywhere, no logos, no brand names visible.
